issue_summary: Deprecate IOUtils.readFully(InputStream, int) in favor of toByteArray(InputStream, int)
javadoc_before:
/** * Reads the requested number of bytes or fail if there are not enough left. *

* This allows for the possibility that {@link InputStream#read(byte[], int, int)} may * not read as many bytes as requested (most likely because of reaching EOF). *

* * @param input where to read input from. * @param length length to read, must be >= 0. * @return the bytes read from input. * @throws IOException if there is a problem reading the file. * @throws IllegalArgumentException if length is negative. * @throws EOFException if the number of bytes read was incorrect. * @since 2.5 */
javadoc_after:
/** * Reads the requested number of bytes or fail if there are not enough left. *

* This allows for the possibility that {@link InputStream#read(byte[], int, int)} may * not read as many bytes as requested (most likely because of reaching EOF). *

* * @param input where to read input from. * @param length length to read, must be >= 0. * @return the bytes read from input. * @throws IOException if there is a problem reading the file. * @throws IllegalArgumentException if length is negative. * @throws EOFException if the number of bytes read was incorrect. * @since 2.5 * @deprecated Use {@link #toByteArray(InputStream, int)}. */
